---
title: "02 · Combine"
subtitle: "One harmonized pigmentation-GWAS compendium"
---

## What this workbook produces

**Output B** — `output/pigmentation_gwas_harmonized.csv`: the catalog pull (Output A) plus
every per-source file in `output/sources/`, all on the shared schema, unioned and
deduplicated by rsID. This step is a **pure union/dedup** — every source is already mapped
onto the common columns in its own workbook, so nothing source-specific happens here except
choosing which source wins a shared rsID.

Set `CATALOG_VERSION` to a filename under `output/catalog/versions/` to pin a specific
catalog pull; the default `"latest"` uses the current Output A.

```{python}
import sys, glob
sys.path.insert(0, "scripts")
import pandas as pd
from harmonize import to_harmonized, const, combine, HARMONIZED_COLS

CATALOG_VERSION = "latest"   # or e.g. "pigmentation_gwas_catalog_20260701T002827Z.csv"

cat_path = ("output/catalog/pigmentation_gwas_catalog.csv" if CATALOG_VERSION == "latest"
            else f"output/catalog/versions/{CATALOG_VERSION}")
cat_raw = pd.read_csv(cat_path, dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)
```

## Map the catalog onto the shared schema

The catalog's `or_beta` column mixes odds ratios and linear betas without a clean unit, and
the v1 pull did not capture the discovery-sample ancestry or the numeric CI. So catalog rows
carry `effect_type = "unknown"` and `needs_sumstats = True` — an explicit flag that these
loci need full summary statistics (or a widened pull) before they are scoring-ready, not a
silent gap. `effect_size` still carries the reported `or_beta` and `eaf` the risk-allele
frequency, so nothing is thrown away.

```{python}
cat_map = {
    "rsid":            "rsid",
    "chr":             "chr",
    "pos":             "pos_hg38",
    "build":           const("hg38"),
    "effect_allele":   "risk_allele",
    "eaf":             "risk_freq",
    "effect_type":     const("unknown"),
    "effect_size":     "or_beta",
    "pvalue":          "pvalue",
    "trait":           "mapped_trait",
    "mapped_gene":     "mapped_gene",
    "pubmed":          "pubmed",
    "study_accession": "study_accession",
}
cat_ver = cat_raw["queried_utc"].iloc[0] if "queried_utc" in cat_raw and len(cat_raw) else "unknown"
catalog = to_harmonized(cat_raw, cat_map, source="gwas_catalog",
                        source_version=cat_ver, needs_sumstats=True)
print(f"catalog: {len(catalog)} loci (queried {cat_ver})")
```

## Union all sources and deduplicate

Precedence prefers African-ancestry paper effect sizes over the catalog for shared rsIDs
(mirroring the source-of-truth choice the SAPPHIRE pipeline made). A shared rsID keeps the
higher-precedence row and its `source` is rewritten to the `+`-joined set of contributing
sources (e.g. `crawford2017+gwas_catalog`), so multi-source provenance survives the dedup.

```{python}
frames = [catalog]
for f in sorted(glob.glob("output/sources/*_harmonized.csv")):
    frames.append(pd.read_csv(f, dtype=str, keep_default_na=False)[HARMONIZED_COLS])

PRECEDENCE = ["crawford2017", "gwas_catalog"]   # earlier = wins a shared rsID
compendium = combine(frames, precedence=PRECEDENCE)
compendium.to_csv("output/pigmentation_gwas_harmonized.csv", index=False)
print(f"{len(compendium)} unique loci -> output/pigmentation_gwas_harmonized.csv")
```

## Summary

```{python}
print("rows per source tag:")
print(compendium["source"].value_counts().to_string())
print(f"\nloci flagged needs_sumstats: {(compendium['needs_sumstats'].astype(str) == 'True').sum()}"
      f" / {len(compendium)}")
compendium.head(15)
```
